description: Research state-of-the-art AI agent orchestration frameworks (e.g., AutoGen, CrewAI, LangGraph, OpenAI Swarm, MetaGPT) and multi-agent collaboration patterns. Also research developments with gastown and openclaw and how they relate to agent orchestration. Document findings in docs/research-notes.md including: key architectural patterns, how they handle agent communication, task decomposition strategies, and feedback loops. Identify at least 5 concrete ideas that could be integrated into wreck-it to advance its orchestration capabilities.

Research emerging patterns in AI-driven code development tools (e.g., Devin, SWE-Agent, OpenHands, Aider, Cursor agent mode) and developments with gastown and openclaw focusing on: how they handle long-running autonomous coding sessions, self-healing/retry strategies, context management across iterations, and quality feedback mechanisms. Document findings in docs/research-notes.md and propose at least 5 actionable improvements for wreck-it's Ralph Wiggum loop.

Based on the research documented in docs/research-notes.md (including findings on gastown and openclaw), create a prioritized roadmap of new features and improvements for wreck-it. Add new task entries to dogfood-tasks.json (as additional tasks in phase 4 or higher with appropriate depends_on referencing existing eval tasks) for each proposed improvement. Focus on features that would make wreck-it a breakthrough tool: dynamic task generation by agents, inter-agent communication, intelligent task routing based on agent specialization, self-improving loop strategies, and integration opportunities with gastown and openclaw.

Implement a role-based agent system in wreck-it. Add a 'role' field to the Task struct in src/types.rs that can be one of: 'ideas' (research and generate tasks), 'implementer' (write code), 'evaluator' (review and validate). Update the task_manager.rs to filter and select tasks by role. Update the serialization to include the role field in tasks.json. Ensure backward compatibility: tasks without a role field default to 'implementer'. Add unit tests for the new role field.

Implement dynamic task generation capability: allow agents to programmatically add new tasks to the task file during execution. Add a method to TaskManager that appends new tasks to the JSON file with proper ID generation (using a prefix + incrementing number pattern). Add a mechanism in the ralph_loop.rs to detect newly added tasks after each iteration and incorporate them into the task queue. Include safeguards: max total tasks limit, validation of task structure, and prevention of circular dependencies. Add unit tests.

Implement an agent context/memory system that persists across iterations. Create a new src/agent_memory.rs module that maintains a per-task context file (.wreck-it-memory/{task_id}.md) containing: previous attempts and their outcomes, key decisions made, relevant code locations discovered, and learnings from failed attempts. Integrate this with the AgentClient so context is included in agent prompts. This enables agents to learn from previous iterations rather than starting fresh each time. Add unit tests.

Implement intelligent task routing and scheduling in ralph_loop.rs. Instead of simple phase-based ordering, add a scoring system that considers: task priority, estimated complexity, dependency satisfaction, number of previous failed attempts, and time since last attempt. Add a TaskScheduler struct that encapsulates this logic and returns an ordered list of ready tasks. This replaces the simple ready_task_indices method with a smarter selection strategy. Add unit tests.

Evaluate the role-based agent system (impl-1) and dynamic task generation (impl-2) implementations. Verify: 1) The role field is properly serialized/deserialized with backward compatibility, 2) Tasks can be dynamically added during execution without corrupting the task file, 3) Circular dependency detection works correctly, 4) All new unit tests pass. Run 'cargo test' and 'cargo clippy' to confirm code quality. Document any issues found in the task descriptions of new fix tasks if needed.

Evaluate the agent memory system (impl-3) and intelligent task scheduling (impl-4) implementations. Verify: 1) Memory files are created and updated correctly across iterations, 2) Memory context is properly injected into agent prompts, 3) The task scheduler produces correct orderings for various scenarios, 4) Edge cases are handled (empty task list, all failed tasks, circular scoring). Run 'cargo test' and 'cargo clippy'. Document any issues found.

Perform end-to-end integration evaluation of all new features working together. Create a small test scenario with a mix of ideas, implementer, and evaluator tasks that exercise: role-based routing, dynamic task generation, agent memory persistence, and intelligent scheduling. Verify the complete workflow runs without errors in headless mode. Run the full test suite with 'cargo test --verbose'. Update docs/architecture.md to reflect the new agent swarm capabilities. Mark this task complete only if all systems work together correctly.

Implement LLM-powered dynamic task planning: add a 'wreck-it plan --goal "..."' sub-command (and an optional pre-loop planning phase in 'wreck-it run --goal "..."') that sends a natural-language goal to the Copilot SDK and receives back a structured task plan as a JSON array. The planner prompt must instruct the model to emit tasks with 'id', 'description', 'phase', and optional 'depends_on' fields. Validate the output against the Task schema before writing it to tasks.json. Add a 'generate_task_plan(goal: &str) -> Result<Vec<Task>>' method to AgentClient and a corresponding 'Plan' variant to the Commands enum in cli.rs. Add unit tests for the schema validation and ID-uniqueness checks.

Implement a critic-actor reflection loop inspired by Openclaw and the Reflexion paper. After an actor agent completes a task and before committing, run a lightweight 'critic' prompt that reads the git diff and evaluates it against the original task description. The critic returns a structured CriticResult { score: f32, issues: Vec<String>, approved: bool }. If approved is false and there are substantive issues, re-invoke the actor with the issues as additional context (up to a configurable --reflection-rounds limit, default 2). Only after the critic approves (or rounds are exhausted) should tests run. Add 'critique_diff(diff: &str, task: &Task) -> Result<CriticResult>' and 'execute_task_with_reflection(task: &Task, rounds: u8) -> Result<()>' to AgentClient. Integrate into ralph_loop.rs. Add unit tests for the reflection round-counting logic.

Implement adaptive re-planning on failure inspired by LangGraph's re-planner node and MetaGPT's iterative planning. After a configurable number of consecutive task failures (--replan-threshold, default 2), invoke a 're-planner' agent that receives: the full current task list, the failed task's description and error output, and the current git status. The re-planner may (a) rewrite the failed task's description, (b) split it into smaller sub-tasks with new IDs, or (c) inject a new prerequisite task. The modified task list must be validated (no duplicate IDs, no new circular dependencies) and persisted to the task file before the loop continues. Add a 'replan(tasks: &[Task], failed: &Task, error: &str) -> Result<Vec<Task>>' method to AgentClient. Integrate into ralph_loop.rs with the threshold check. Add unit tests for the circular-dependency guard and ID-deduplication.

Implement a typed artefact store and context chain inspired by MetaGPT artefacts and CrewAI's task context field. Extend the Task struct in types.rs with optional 'inputs: Vec<String>' (referencing 'task-id/artefact-name') and 'outputs: Vec<TaskArtefact>' fields, where TaskArtefact has 'kind: ArtefactKind (file | json | summary)', 'name: String', and 'path: String'. When a task completes, persist declared output artefacts into an artefact manifest stored alongside .wreck-it-state.json. Before executing a task, resolve its 'inputs' references by reading the manifest and injecting the artefact contents into the agent's prompt context. Ensure backward compatibility: tasks without inputs/outputs fields behave identically to today. Add unit tests for artefact serialisation, resolution, and missing-artefact error handling.

Implement a Gastown cloud runtime integration. Add a 'gastown' ModelProvider variant (or a separate 'runtime' field on Task) so tasks can declare 'runtime: gastown' to offload execution to a gastown cloud agent service. Implement a GastownClient in a new src/gastown_client.rs module that: (1) serialises the wreck-it task graph as a gastown-compatible workflow DAG (YAML/JSON), (2) submits the workflow to a configurable gastown endpoint, (3) polls for task completion events and updates .wreck-it-state.json accordingly. Add gastown_endpoint and gastown_token configuration fields to Config in types.rs (both optional; when absent, gastown integration is disabled). Add unit tests for DAG serialisation and status-update handling. Document the integration in docs/roadmap.md.

Implement Openclaw provenance tracking integration. During task execution in ralph_loop.rs, record a provenance record for every agent invocation: { task_id, agent_role, model, prompt_hash, tool_calls: [...], git_diff_hash, timestamp, outcome }. Persist provenance records to .wreck-it-provenance/<task_id>.json. Add a 'wreck-it provenance --task <id>' sub-command that prints the human-readable provenance chain for a task. Add a 'wreck-it export-openclaw [--output path]' sub-command that serialises the full run provenance in the openclaw plan-graph JSON format for import into the openclaw visualiser. Add unit tests for provenance record serialisation and the export format. Document the integration in docs/roadmap.md.

Evaluate the LLM-powered dynamic task planning (impl-5) and critic-actor reflection loop (impl-6) implementations. Verify: 1) 'wreck-it plan --goal' generates a valid tasks.json with correct schema, unique IDs, and sensible dependency structure for a realistic goal; 2) The critic prompt correctly identifies an intentionally flawed diff (e.g., missing function body, wrong file edited) and triggers a reflection round; 3) The reflection round counter is respected and the loop does not spin infinitely; 4) All new unit tests pass with 'cargo test'; 5) 'cargo clippy -- -D warnings' reports no issues. Document any defects found as new fix tasks.

Evaluate the adaptive re-planning on failure (impl-7) and typed artefact store (impl-8) implementations. Verify: 1) After exceeding the replan threshold, the re-planner agent is invoked and produces a modified task list that is persisted correctly; 2) Circular dependency and duplicate ID guards reject invalid re-planner outputs; 3) Artefact outputs are persisted after task completion and correctly injected into downstream task prompts; 4) Tasks without inputs/outputs fields behave identically to pre-impl-8 behaviour (backward compatibility); 5) All new unit tests pass with 'cargo test'; 6) 'cargo clippy -- -D warnings' reports no issues. Document any defects found as new fix tasks.

Evaluate the Gastown cloud runtime integration (impl-9) and Openclaw provenance tracking integration (impl-10). Verify: 1) The task graph is correctly serialised as a gastown-compatible workflow DAG; 2) When gastown_endpoint is absent in config, the gastown runtime is silently disabled and wreck-it falls back to local execution; 3) Provenance records are written to .wreck-it-provenance/ for each task execution; 4) 'wreck-it provenance --task <id>' correctly prints the provenance chain; 5) 'wreck-it export-openclaw' produces valid openclaw plan-graph JSON; 6) All new unit tests pass with 'cargo test'; 7) 'cargo clippy -- -D warnings' reports no issues. Document any defects found as new fix tasks.

Perform full end-to-end integration evaluation of all Horizon 2 and Horizon 3 features (impl-5 through impl-10) working together. Design a realistic scenario: provide a natural-language goal to 'wreck-it plan', let role-based routing assign specialist agents, observe critic-actor reflection improving output quality, trigger a deliberate failure to test adaptive re-planning, verify artefact chaining passes context between tasks, confirm provenance records are written for every step, and export the full run to openclaw format. Run 'cargo test --verbose' to confirm no regressions. Update docs/architecture.md to document the complete feature set. This task is the acceptance gate for the entire Horizon 2–3 roadmap.
